ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch. Richard Collier for Dennis McGuire. Christine Carevich in Propria Persona. Deidre Mack (standing in for Dennis McGuire) also present.

Ms. Carevich stated that she filed a petition for approval of acts under power of attorney and for compensation with a hearing date of February 24, 2015. Ms. Mack stated that she received letter from Mr. McGuire yesterday giving her permission to sign a change of address, and stating that he wants the new address to be used and that he does not want funds being mailed to the institution (issue left in Mr. Der's hands). Mr. Der stated that he needs to look into the issue of whether the Victim's Compensation Board needs to be noticed of the change of address. Ms. Mack also stated that Mr. McGuire requested a one week continuance to file an addendum to the objection and requested to withdraw the motion for Court order under the condition that Mr. Collier state for the record that he signed the substitution of attorney. The Court noted that the substitution of attorney was signed by Mr. McGuire as a Beneficiary and that it speaks for itself. The motion for court order is denied. The accounting petition is under submission as of December 9, 2014 to see if an addendum is filed. The Court stated that it is not inclined to approve a set aside for Ms. Carevich's claim.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-04-2014 Continued To Nov-18-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard James Collier appearing. Ernest Der appearing for Debra Dolch. Deirdre Mack (Friend of Dennis McGuire) appearing. Christine Carevich appearing in pro per.

Mr. Collier addressed his standing as counsel for Dennis McGuire stating that he substituted out as counsel of record with regard to his representation of Mr. McGuire as a beneficiary only - not as personal representative. The Court stated that it had reviewed the file, that Mr. McGuire filed documents on 10/31/14, and that additional papers were submitted but returned because they were not compliant with the Rules of Court. Ms. Carevich addressed the Court briefly and requested that the Court review her documents. The Court stated that it could not accept unfiled documents and that if the Court is to review any document it must be filed and served on all parties. Ms. Mack addressed Mr. McGuire's filings. The Court stated that one filing was mistakenly returned but that it was filed at a later time. In addition, the Court stated that a filing was returned/rejected because Mr. McGuire did not include a filing fee and that he didn't have a court date. The Court further stated that it treated that rejected filing as a request for a continunace. Ms. Mack stated that Mr. McGuire wants to proceed, that he has concerns about his filings being rejected, and that he may need to get another lawyer. Mr. Der stated that the matter should go forward and that the real issue is with Ms. Carevich's claims/objections. Mr. Collier argued in support of his fees as requested. Counsel noted that Mr. McGuire is not waiving statutory fees but is waiving extraordinary fees. The Court stated that it had read all the papers, that all filings must comply with the Rules of Court/Local Rules, and that Mr. McGuire has time to proceed, In addition, the Court stated that it would not be inclined to increase the reserve, that there have been numerous delays, and that it was granting one final continuace. The Court stated that if there was anything else to present that the parties need to get their documents on file with the Court. The Court then continued the matter to November 18, 2014 at 9:00 am in Department 204.
Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition By Executor Dennis V. Mcguire For Preliminary Distribution And Partial Payment Of Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Jun-26-2013 Continued To Sep-04-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Collier for Dennis McGuire. Christine Carevich in Propria Persona.

Mr. Collier explained that Ms. Carevich wants to put in a request to be paid for services, that she has been very helpful in collecting records/documents and that Mr. McGuire is not willing to pay her. The Court informed Mr. Collier that an inventory and appraisal is required prior to distribution and payment of fees. Mr. Collier also stated that there was a Notice of Proposed Action when the property sold, that the house was in very bad shape, that there was less than $30,000.00 in the bank account, that it all went into cleaning up the house and that it was in an account named in the Estate earning interest. In addition, Mr. Collier stated that Mr. McGuire refused to sign the inventory and appraisal he prepared but that he will try again, that no Probate Referee is appointed, that a date of death appraisal was obtained, and that there is the remaining issue of Ms. Carevich and the Grasp Valley property (possible contamination and need for an environmental inspection). The Court stated that the Estate is not in a position for a preliminary distribution, expressed concern about Mr. McGuire's ability to fulfill his fiduciary duties as the Personal Representative, stated that it is inclined to suspend him and that fees are in a blocked amount and not itemized. Mr. Collier stated that he does not know what the charges are against Mr. McGuire or when he will be released. The Court suspended Mr. McGuire's powers immediately. The Court asked Mr. Collier to file an Inventory and Appraisal and a Request for Appointment of A Probate Referee, to consider whether a Professional Fiduciary ought to come in and who the Fiduciary should be, whether Mr. McGuire will consent to a Professional Fiduciary and file an itemization of his (Mr. Collier's) fees. The Petition for Preliminary Distribution and Partial Payment of Attorney's Fees was continued to September 4, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. This is a Court requested continuance.
